name: Fuensanta Reservoir and Monte Ardal Loop
name_original: Vuelta al embalse de la Fuensanta desde Yeste
description: This difficult 24.9-mile touring cycling route from Yeste offers 2685 feet of climbing around the scenic Fuensanta Reservoir.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Yeste traverse varied terrain, including the Sierra de Segura mountains and the scenic Valle del Río Segura. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often passing through natural areas like the Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima. Cyclists can expect a mix of well-paved and unpaved surfaces, with many routes presenting a challenging profile. The landscape is characterized by natural features such as the Embalse de la Fuensanta reservoir and the Cerro de Rala Micro-reserve. Best touring cycling routes around Yeste The most popular touring cycling route is Monte Ardal and Calares del Mundo Park Loop, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 3,700 feet (1,100 meters) of elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Borriqueta and Monte Ardal Loop, a difficult 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This route offers extensive climbs and descents through the Monte Ardal area, providing panoramic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Cerro de Rala Micro-reserve Loop from Yeste, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail leading through the Cerro de Rala Micro-reserve, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Yeste is defined by mountainous landscapes, the Fuensanta Reservoir, and the Valle del Río Segura. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists due to significant elevation and route lengths. The routes in Yeste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Yeste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Yeste?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Yeste, primarily catering to experienced cyclists due to significant elevation changes and route lengths. The region offers a diverse network of paths through mountainous landscapes and natural parks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Yeste?
A: While Yeste's touring cycling routes are generally challenging with significant elevation gains, there is one moderate route available. Most of the 17 other routes are classified as difficult, making them less suitable for families with young children or beginner cyclists. These routes often feature varied terrain, including unpaved sections.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Yeste?
A: Touring cycling routes in Yeste feature varied terrain, including both well-paved and unpaved surfaces. You can expect significant elevation changes as routes traverse mountainous areas like the Sierra de Segura and the Monte Ardal area. The landscape also includes scenic valleys and reservoir surroundings.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling in Yeste?
A: Cyclists in Yeste can enjoy panoramic views and unique geological formations. Routes often pass through the scenic Valle del Río Segura, near the picturesque Embalse de la Fuensanta (Fuensanta Reservoir), and through natural areas like the Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065333063/cerro-de-rala-micro-reserve-loop-from-yeste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Rala Micro-reserve Loop from Yeste&lt;/a&gt;, for example, takes you through a significant micro-reserve.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Yeste area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Yeste are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363262902/monte-ardal-and-calares-del-mundo-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Ardal and Calares del Mundo Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363168218/la-borriqueta-and-monte-ardal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Borriqueta and Monte Ardal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both providing extensive climbs and descents with panoramic views.

Q: What attractions can I explore near the cycling routes in Yeste?
A: Beyond the cycling trails, Yeste offers several attractions. You can visit the historic Castillo de Yeste, which is featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption – Yeste Castle loop from Yeste&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include various waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saltaor Waterfall (Zurriero)&lt;/a&gt;. Other cultural sites like Corredera Plaza and San Bartolome Chapel are also accessible.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Yeste?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential colder temperatures or adverse weather in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before planning your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Fuensanta Reservoir?
A: Yes, the scenic Fuensanta Reservoir is a recurring highlight in cycling itineraries around Yeste. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363254972/fuensanta-reservoir-and-monte-ardal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuensanta Reservoir and Monte Ardal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that offers picturesque backdrops along its path.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Yeste?
A: The touring cycling routes in Yeste are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Yeste's varied terrain, often praising the challenging profiles and the stunning natural beauty of the mountainous landscapes.

Q: Are there accommodations in Yeste that cater to cyclists?
A: The region offers rural accommodations, many of which are geared towards outdoor activities like hiking and biking. These establishments often provide a convenient base for exploring the area's touring cycling routes.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes in Yeste suitable for long-distance rides?
A: The routes around Yeste typically range from approximately 30 to 50 kilometers (18 to 32 miles) in length, often featuring significant elevation gains. While not ultra-long distance, these routes provide substantial day-long challenges for touring cyclists looking for extended rides through varied and demanding terrain.
tips: Nice bridge over the Tús river that feeds the Fuensanta reservoir. Stop for a while to take some photos of the turquoise waters of the swamp.
View from the road that passes above the reservoir with views of its waters and curious "islets".
Bridge over the Segura River, with a very interesting and photogenic shape, also with great views of the Fuensanta reservoir. The bridge was built in 2007.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 50 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for touring bicycles and is described as being mostly paved and well-maintained. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved or more challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike in certain sections, especially if you venture onto harder variants.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a 'difficult' touring bicycle ride, requiring 'very good fitness'. With a significant elevation gain of over 800 meters and a distance of 40 kilometers, it is best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful views of the Sierra Segura region and the Fuensanta Reservoir. You'll also pass by notable structures such as the Palomares Bridge and the Vicaría Bridge. A highlight is the View of the Fuensanta Reservoir itself, known for its distinctive V-shape where the Segura and Tus rivers meet.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the route near Yeste is conveniently located close to a parking lot, making access straightforward for cyclists arriving by car.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations are not detailed, the Sierra Segura region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not specifically state whether this particular cycling route is dog-friendly. As a general rule for touring bicycle routes, dogs are often allowed if they can comfortably keep pace or are carried in a suitable trailer. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash and you clean up after them, especially when passing through natural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Microrreserva Ardal y Tinjarra and is within the broader Sierra de Segura Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public routes within these natural areas. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This specific Komoot route, 'Return to the Fuensanta reservoir from Yeste', is designed as a loop trail, starting and ending near Yeste. This allows you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other official trails. You will find small portions that overlap with the [CIMA AB07] Monte Ardal * Yeste, La Cruz de San Cristobal, and the GR-68.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yeste, being the starting and ending point, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the town to refuel and rest before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check availability and opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.
